message.content:
Scan the OpenCoreAnalysisKit codebase and generate a complete project inventory including:
- All source files with detected languages
- Build system and dependencies
- Import/dependency maps
- Complexity estimates

Focus on the parser/ and android/ directories which contain the main implementation.

Output the scan results to docs/exploration/00-project-scan.json

attachment.content:
- coredump: Analyze an Android coredump file. Print a Java class and its members, or drill into any field by address.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- explore-opencore-codebase: 深度探索 OpenCoreAnalysisKit 代码库并构建知识图谱
- claude-mem:babysit: Watch a pull request or review cycle until it is ready to merge. Use when asked to babysit, monitor, or keep checking PR comments, reviews, and CI until all actionable issues are resolved.
- claude-mem:design-is: Audit a design against Dieter Rams' ten "Good design is..." principles, then hand off a /make-plan prompt for one of three outcomes — new design, refine design, or redesign. Use when the user says "audit this design", "design review", "check this UI against Rams", "is this UI good", "critique this design", "design audit", or asks for a critique that should lead to a plan.
- claude-mem:do: Execute a phased implementation plan using subagents. Use when asked to execute, run, or carry out a plan — especially one created by make-plan.
- claude-mem:how-it-works
- claude-mem:knowledge-agent
- claude-mem:learn-codebase
- claude-mem:make-plan
- claude-mem:mem-search
- claude-mem:oh-my-issues
- claude-mem:pathfinder
- claude-mem:smart-explore
- claude-mem:timeline-report
- claude-mem:version-bump
- claude-mem:weekly-digests
- claude-mem:wowerpoint
- understand-anything:understand: Analyze a codebase to produce an interactive knowledge graph for understanding architecture, components, and relationships
- understand-anything:understand-chat
- understand-anything:understand-dashboard
- understand-anything:understand-diff
- understand-anything:understand-domain
- understand-anything:understand-explain
- understand-anything:understand-knowledge
- understand-anything:understand-onboard
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.text:
我将扫描 OpenCoreAnalysisKit 项目并生成完整的项目清单。按照标准流程分三步执行：

## Phase 1 Step A - 读取项目清单文件

content.content:
1	# Dependencies
2	[Capstone Disassembly Framework](https://github.com/capstone-engine/capstone)
3	[XZ Utils](https://github.com/tukaani-project/xz)
4	[HttpLib](https://github.com/yhirose/cpp-httplib)
5	[Zstd](https://github.com/facebook/zstd)
6	
7	# Techincal System
8	![core-analysis](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Penguin38/OpenCoreAnalysisKit/refs/heads/main/doc/OpenCoreAnalyzer.jpg)
9	
10	| Project      | Path                                              |
11	|:------------:|---------------------------------------------------|
12	|core-parser   | https://github.com/Penguin38/OpenCoreAnalysisKit  |
13	|linux-parser  | https://github.com/Penguin38/OpenLinuxAnalysisKit |
14	|crash-android | https://github.com/Penguin38/crash-android        |
15	|OpenCoreSDK   | https://github.com/Penguin38/OpenCoreSDK          |

1	# Copyright (C) 2024-present, Guanyou.Chen. All rights reserved.
2	#

11	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
12	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
13	# limitations under the License.
14	
15	cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.21.1)
16	project(OpenCoreAnalysisKit)
17	set(TARGET_CORE_PARSER core-parser)
18	add_definitions(-D__CORE_PARSER__="${TARGET_CORE_PARSER}")
19	
20	file(READ ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/VERSION version_contents)
21	string(REGEX MATCH "MAJOR=([0-9]+)" _ ${version_contents})
22	set(MAJOR ${CMAKE_MATCH_1})
23	string(REGEX MATCH "MINOR=([0-9]+)" _ ${version_contents})
24	set(MINOR ${CMAKE_MATCH_1})
25	string(REGEX MATCH "PATCH=([0-9]+)" _ ${version_contents})
26	set(PATCH ${CMAKE_MATCH_1})
27	add_definitions(-D__PARSER_VERSION__="${MAJOR}.${MINOR}.${PATCH}")
28	
29	function(get_git_revision_variable VAR)
30	    find_package(Git QUIET)
31	    if(GIT_FOUND AND EXISTS "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/.git")
32	        execute_process(
33	            COMMAND ${GIT_EXECUTABLE} describe --tags --always --dirty
34	            WORKING_DIRECTORY ${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}
35	            OUTPUT_VARIABLE GIT_VERSION
36	            ERROR_QUIET
37	            OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE
38	        )
39	        set(${VAR} "${GIT_VERSION}" PARENT_SCOPE)
40	    else()
41	        set(${VAR} "unknown" PARENT_SCOPE)
42	    endif()
43	endfunction()
44	get_git_revision_variable(GIT_VERSION)
45	add_definitions(-D__GIT_VERSION__="${GIT_VERSION}")
46	
47	string(TOUPPER "${CMAKE_BUILD_PRODUCT}" TARGET_BUILD_PRODUCT)
48	add_definitions(-D__${TARGET_BUILD_PRODUCT}_PARSER__)
49	add_definitions(-D__TARGET_PARSER__="${CMAKE_BUILD_PRODUCT}")
50	
51	string(TOUPPER "${CMAKE_BUILD_TARGET}" TARGET_BUILD_PLATFORM)
52	add_definitions(-D__${TARGET_BUILD_PLATFORM}__)
53	
54	if (ANDROID_ABI STREQUAL "arm64-v8a")
55	add_definitions(-D__ARM64__)
56	add_definitions(-D__LP64__)
57	elseif(ANDROID_ABI STREQUAL "armeabi-v7a")
58	add_definitions(-D__ARM__)
59	add_definitions(-D__LP32__)
60	elseif(ANDROID_ABI STREQUAL "armeabi")
61	add_definitions(-D__ARM__)
62	add_definitions(-D__LP32__)
63	elseif(ANDROID_ABI STREQUAL "x86_64")
64	add_definitions(-D__X86_64__)
65	add_definitions(-D__LP64__)
66	elseif(ANDROID_ABI STREQUAL "x86")
67	add_definitions(-D__X86__)
68	add_definitions(-D__LP32__)
69	elseif(ANDROID_ABI STREQUAL "riscv64")
70	add_definitions(-D__RISCV64__)
71	add_definitions(-D__LP64__)
72	endif()
73	
74	if(CMAKE_SIZEOF_VOID_P EQUAL 8)
75	add_definitions(-D__LP64__)
76	elseif(CMAKE_SIZEOF_VOID_P EQUAL 4)
77	add_definitions(-D__LP32__)
78	else()
79	add_definitions(-D__LP64__)
80	endif()
81

83	add_definitions(-D__PARSER_${TARGET_BUILD_TYPE}__)
84

404	    parser/command/fake/core/lp32/fake_core.cpp

414	    parser/command/fake/core/x86/tombstone_parser.cpp

420	    parser/command/fake/exec/fake_executable_32.cpp
421	    parser/command/fake/load/fake_load_block.cpp
422	    parser/command/fake/stack/fake_java_stack.cpp)
423	
424	if (NOT TARGET_BUILD_PLATFORM STREQUAL "MACOS" AND NOT TARGET_BUILD_PLATFORM STREQUAL "WINDOWS")
425	    list(APPEND CORE_PARSER_SOURCES
426	        parser/command/remote/cmd_remote.cpp
427	        parser/command/remote/opencore/opencore.cpp
428	        parser/command/remote/opencore/lp64/opencore.cpp
429	        parser/command/remote/opencore/lp32/opencore.cpp
430	        parser/command/remote/opencore/arm64/opencore.cpp
431	        parser/command/remote/opencore/arm/opencore.cpp
432	        parser/command/remote/opencore/x86_64/opencore.cpp
433	        parser/command/remote/opencore/x86/opencore.cpp
434	        parser/command/remote/opencore/riscv64/opencore.cpp
435	        parser/command/remote/fakecore/process.cpp
436	        parser/command/remote/fakecore/arm64/process_parser.cpp
437	        parser/command/remote/fakecore/arm/process_parser.cpp
438	        parser/command/remote/fakecore/x86_64/process_parser.cpp
439	        parser/command/remote/fakecore/x86/process_parser.cpp
440	        parser/command/remote/fakecore/riscv64/process_parser.cpp
441	        parser/command/remote/hook/hook.cpp
442	        parser/command/remote/hook/arm64/hook.cpp
443	        parser/command/remote/hook/arm/hook.cpp
444	        parser/command/remote/hook/x86_64/hook.cpp
445	        parser/command/remote/hook/x86/hook.cpp
446	        parser/command/remote/hook/riscv64/hook.cpp
447	        parser/command/remote/setprop/property.cpp
448	        parser/command/remote/setprop/prop_area.cpp)
449	endif()
450	
451	add_library(parser STATIC ${CORE_PARSER_SOURCES})
452	target_link_libraries(parser android llvm core utils)
453	
454	set(HTTPLIB_INCLUDE_DIR ${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/3rd-party/cpp-httplib)
455	include_directories(${HTTPLIB_INCLUDE_DIR})
456	if(CMAKE_SIZEOF_VOID_P EQUAL 8)
457	add_definitions(-D__HTTPLIB__)
458	endif()
459	
460	# Embed parser/daemon/http/index.html into a generated header index_html.h
461	set(INDEX_HTML_INPUT  ${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/parser/daemon/http/index.html)
462	set(INDEX_HTML_OUTPUT ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/index_html.h)
463	add_custom_command(
464	    OUTPUT  ${INDEX_HTML_OUTPUT}
465	    COMMAND ${CMAKE_COMMAND}
466	            -DINPUT=${INDEX_HTML_INPUT}
467	            -DOUTPUT=${INDEX_HTML_OUTPUT}
468	            -DVAR_NAME=IndexHtml
469	            -P ${CMAKE_CURRENT_LIST_DIR}/cmake/EmbedFile.cmake
470	    DEPENDS ${INDEX_HTML_INPUT}
471	    COMMENT "Embedding index.html -> index_html.h"
472	)
473	add_custom_target(gen_index_html DEPENDS ${INDEX_HTML_OUTPUT})
474	include_directories(${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR})
475	
476	if (TARGET_BUILD_PLATFORM STREQUAL "WINDOWS")
477	set(MINI_SHELL_SRC parser/ui/windows/mini_shell.cpp)
478	else()
479	set(MINI_SHELL_SRC parser/ui/linux/mini_shell.cpp)
480	endif()
481	
482	add_executable(${TARGET_CORE_PARSER}
483	               parser/ui/ui_thread.cpp
484	               ${MINI_SHELL_SRC}
485	               parser/work/work_thread.cpp
486	               parser/core_parser.cpp
487	               parser/daemon/http/server.cpp
488	               parser/daemon/mcp/server.cpp)
489	target_link_libraries(${TARGET_CORE_PARSER} parser)
490	add_dependencies(${TARGET_CORE_PARSER} gen_index_html)
491	if (TARGET_BUILD_PAGESIZE STREQUAL "16K")
492	target_link_options(${TARGET_CORE_PARSER} PRIVATE "-Wl,-z,max-page-size=16384")
493	endif()
494	
495	add_executable(test tests/test.cpp)
496	target_link_libraries(test parser)
497
